TypeSignature.Value: .class public sequential ansi sealed beforefieldinit SKFourByteTag extends System.ValueType implements class System.IEquatable`1<valuetype SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag>

Docs.summary: Represents a four-byte tag, packing four ASCII characters into a single 32-bit integer.

remarks.format:
## Remarks
`SKFourByteTag` represents a four-byte identifier used extensively in OpenType fonts, ICC color profiles, and other binary formats. The tag packs four ASCII characters into a single 32-bit integer: the first character occupies the most-significant byte.
You can construct a tag from four characters, from a `uint`, or by parsing a string. Implicit conversions to and from `uint` are provided for convenience.
This is a value type and does not require disposal.
## Examples
```csharp // Construct the 'wght' (weight) axis tag var wght = SKFourByteTag.Parse("wght");
// Use as a uint uint raw = wght2; Console.WriteLine(raw.ToString("X8")); // 77676874 ```

.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(unsigned int32 value) cil managed

Docs.param: The raw 32-bit unsigned integer value of the tag.

.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(char c1, char c2, char c3, char c4) cil managed

Docs.param: The first (most significant) character of the tag.
The second character of the tag.
The third character of the tag.
The fourth (least significant) character of the tag.
Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct from four characters.

MemberSignature.Value: public static SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag Parse (string? tag);
.method public static hidebysig valuetype SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag Parse(string tag) cil managed

Docs.param: The string to parse. Only the first four characters are used; shorter strings are padded with spaces.
Docs.summary: Parses a string into an by using up to four characters.
Docs.returns: An constructed from the first four characters of , padded with spaces if necessary. Returns a zero-value tag if is or empty.

MemberSignature.Value: public override string ToString ();
.method public hidebysig virtual instance string ToString() cil managed

Docs.summary: Returns a four-character string representation of the tag.
Docs.returns: A four-character string formed by decoding each byte of the tag as an ASCII character.
